content:
Thank you for asking about this. I want to be very clear about what evidence says on this topic.

The claim that lifting your arms above your head during pregnancy will harm the baby is a common myth but is not supported by scientific evidence. There is no credible medical research showing that simply raising your arms above your head causes harm to your baby.

Some people might feel mild discomfort or lightheadedness if they hold their arms up for a long time, due to temporary changes in blood flow or muscle strain, but this does not translate into any harm to the fetus.

Pregnant individuals are generally encouraged to stay active and move comfortably. Of course, if any movement causes pain or severe discomfort, it’s wise to stop and consult a healthcare provider, but lifting your arms above your head in itself is not harmful.

If you have seen sources claiming otherwise, I would be interested in reviewing them to check if they provide any scientific evidence. But based on current medical knowledge, lifting your arms overhead during pregnancy is safe.
